channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading space research institutions. Applications are currently open for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship Program 2026, designed for high school students who are eager to explore careers in STEM. Hosted […]

The post NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading space research institutions. Applications are currently open for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship Program 2026, designed for high school students who are eager to explore careers in STEM.

Hosted at the Goddard Space Flight Center, the program provides students with a chance to work alongside scientists, engineers, and researchers who are developing cutting-edge space and Earth science technologies.

The internship aims to inspire the next generation of innovators by exposing students to real-world projects, mentorship, and collaborative learning environments within the space science community.

Program Overview

The NASA Goddard Summer Internship offers high school students the opportunity to participate in research-based and technology-focused activities during the summer. Interns will gain valuable exposure to scientific methods, engineering practices, and data-driven problem-solving.

Students selected for the program may work on projects related to space science, Earth observation, astrophysics, computer science, or engineering while learning from experienced professionals at NASA.

Benefits of the Internship

Participants in the program will gain several valuable experiences, including:

  • Hands-on exposure to NASA’s research and technology development

  • Mentorship from experienced scientists, engineers, and researchers

  • Practical experience working on real-world STEM projects

  • Opportunities to explore potential career paths in science and engineering

  • Networking with professionals and fellow students interested in space science

The internship serves as an excellent stepping stone for students who aspire to pursue future studies or careers in STEM fields.

Eligibility

The opportunity is open to high school students aged 16 years or older who will live within 50 miles of the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center during the summer program.

Students who are passionate about science, technology, or engineering are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this unique learning experience.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship 2026 is:

April 17, 2026

Students interested in participating should ensure their applications are completed before the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more about the internship and submit their application through the official link below.

Apply here

For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Visit Here.

The post NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Moving Minds Alliance (MMA) 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis (ECDiC) Research Fellowship Program, an initiative designed to support local researchers working to improve the lives of young children and caregivers affected by crisis. This fellowship aims to strengthen research and evidence on children aged 0–8 years and their […]

The post Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Moving Minds Alliance (MMA) 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis (ECDiC) Research Fellowship Program, an initiative designed to support local researchers working to improve the lives of young children and caregivers affected by crisis.

This fellowship aims to strengthen research and evidence on children aged 0–8 years and their caregivers living in crisis contexts, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. Through financial support and collaborative opportunities, the program empowers researchers to conduct field-relevant studies that influence policies, programs, and interventions supporting vulnerable families.

The Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship seeks to elevate the voices and expertise of local researchers who are closely connected to the communities they study. By supporting locally driven research, the program contributes to building stronger evidence and solutions for children living in challenging environments.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive several forms of support to help them successfully conduct their research and expand its impact.

These benefits include:

  • A one-time stipend of up to $5,000 to support research activities

  • 12 months of tailored mentorship and guidance while carrying out field-relevant studies

  • Opportunities to collaborate with a global network of researchers focused on early childhood development

  • Engagement with the MMA Research Forum team to strengthen research outputs and amplify findings

This combination of financial assistance, mentorship, and collaboration helps fellows produce impactful research that can influence programs supporting young children and families in crisis settings.

Focus Areas

The fellowship supports research related to Early Childhood Development in Crisis contexts, particularly focusing on:

  • Young children aged 0–8 years

  • Caregivers supporting children in fragile or emergency settings

  • Evidence that can inform policy, humanitarian response, and development programs

Researchers are encouraged to design studies that address real-world challenges affecting children and families living in crisis conditions.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 ECDiC Research Fellowship Program is:

March 31, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the program requirements before submitting their applications.

How to Apply

Interested researchers should review the official Terms of Reference and submit their applications through the link below.

Download the Terms of Reference.

For more Fellowship Opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Young Leaders initiative, an opportunity designed to support young changemakers who are working to improve health, wellbeing, and opportunity for young people around the world. Through a series of Impact Fellowships and scholarships to global youth leadership events, this initiative helps emerging leaders strengthen […]

The post Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Young Leaders initiative, an opportunity designed to support young changemakers who are working to improve health, wellbeing, and opportunity for young people around the world. Through a series of Impact Fellowships and scholarships to global youth leadership events, this initiative helps emerging leaders strengthen their work, expand their networks, and amplify their impact.

Open to individuals aged 18 to 35, the Young Health Programme recognizes that young people are already driving innovative solutions to complex social and health challenges. The initiative provides a platform to support these leaders by connecting them with global partners such as One Young World and the AFS Youth Assembly, two well-known international gatherings that bring together youth leaders, policymakers, and experts from around the world.

Participants selected through the Young Health Programme will have opportunities to attend these global events through fully supported fellowships and scholarships, where they can exchange ideas, develop leadership skills, and collaborate with other young changemakers working to create healthier communities.

About the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme

The Young Health Programme (YHP) is a global initiative led by AstraZeneca that focuses on empowering young people to take action on issues affecting youth health and wellbeing. The program works with partners around the world to support youth-led initiatives, strengthen leadership skills, and help young advocates bring their ideas to larger platforms.

Through the Young Leaders initiative, the program identifies passionate individuals who are already making a difference in their communities. Whether they are working on health education, youth empowerment, community wellbeing, environmental sustainability, or social innovation, the program seeks to support those whose work contributes to healthier and more resilient societies.

Selected participants become part of a growing network of youth advocates who are collaborating to improve the future of health for young people globally.

What the Opportunity Offers

Through the Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative, selected applicants can receive support through several pathways designed to help them grow as leaders and expand the impact of their work.

Participants may receive:

Scholarships to attend major global leadership events. These include gatherings such as the One Young World Summit or the AFS Youth Assembly, where youth leaders connect with experts, organizations, and policymakers from across the world.

Leadership development opportunities. Fellows and scholars gain access to training, mentoring, and learning opportunities designed to strengthen their leadership skills and strategic thinking.

Global networking opportunities. Participants join an international community of changemakers working on youth health and social impact initiatives.

Platforms to amplify their work. Through partnerships and global events, selected young leaders have opportunities to showcase their initiatives and gain visibility for the issues they care about.

These experiences are designed to help young changemakers expand their reach, collaborate with others, and scale their solutions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Young people are increasingly at the forefront of solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges. From improving access to health services to promoting mental wellbeing, environmental sustainability, and social inclusion, youth-led initiatives are shaping the future of communities around the globe.

However, many young leaders lack access to the resources, networks, and platforms that could help them scale their work. Opportunities like the Young Health Programme Fellowships and Scholarships help bridge that gap by connecting youth changemakers with global networks, leadership training, and spaces where their ideas can reach a wider audience.

By investing in youth leadership, programs like this not only support individual changemakers but also help strengthen the global movement toward healthier, more equitable societies.

Who Should Apply

The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative is open to young people aged 18 to 35 who are already taking action to create positive change.

Ideal applicants include individuals who:

  • Are leading or contributing to initiatives that improve youth health or wellbeing
  • Are passionate about social impact, community development, or sustainability
  • Want to strengthen their leadership skills and expand their networks
  • Are eager to collaborate with other global changemakers

Applicants may come from a wide variety of backgrounds, including youth organizations, nonprofits, social enterprises, health advocacy groups, or community initiatives.

How to Apply

Applications for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative are now open. Interested applicants should review the eligibility criteria and available fellowship and scholarship pathways on the official program page.

Because different opportunities may have different timelines and requirements, applicants are encouraged to review all available options carefully before submitting their applications.

You can find full details and begin your application on the official AstraZeneca website.

Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website!

The post Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program: Become a Leader for Social Connection in Your Community
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program, an initiative designed to empower passionate community leaders who are committed to strengthening social connections and fostering belonging in their communities. Across the country, F4SC ambassadors are already creating meaningful change by uplifting community voices, building stronger social ties, and creating spaces where individuals feel […]

The post Apply for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program: Become a Leader for Social Connection in Your Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program, an initiative designed to empower passionate community leaders who are committed to strengthening social connections and fostering belonging in their communities.

Across the country, F4SC ambassadors are already creating meaningful change by uplifting community voices, building stronger social ties, and creating spaces where individuals feel valued and included. The program celebrates leadership, creativity, and collaboration while mobilizing a growing network of changemakers working toward a shared mission.

The Ambassador Program equips participants with the tools, research, and partnerships needed to transform diverse lived experiences into impactful campaigns. Ambassadors work together to shape powerful narratives that highlight the importance of social connection and drive collective action at both local and national levels.

What the Program Offers

Participants in the 2026 Ambassador Program will receive access to:

  • Research-backed resources and communication tools to support community initiatives

  • Opportunities to collaborate with a network of thought leaders and advocates

  • Training and learning sessions focused on storytelling and advocacy

  • Recognition for their leadership and impact in advancing social connection

  • Participation in coordinated communication and awareness campaigns

Through these resources, ambassadors can amplify their voices, influence public conversations, and build stronger relationships within their communities.

Who F4SC Is Looking For

The program welcomes individuals who are passionate about strengthening social bonds and bringing people together. Ideal applicants include:

  • Local Luminaries with strong connections and deep roots in their communities

  • Movement Messengers who want to shape public conversations and inspire change through storytelling

  • Capital Connectors who can build bridges, open opportunities, and foster collaboration

Ambassadors will participate in storytelling initiatives, communications campaigns, and community engagement efforts while contributing to a nationwide movement focused on social connection.

Why Social Connection Matters

Strong communities thrive when people feel connected, valued, and supported. Social connection plays a critical role in improving well-being, strengthening civic engagement, and creating more inclusive societies.

By joining the F4SC Ambassador Program, participants become part of a national effort to highlight the importance of connection and help communities build stronger relationships that foster belonging.

How to Apply

Applications for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program are currently open.

Interested candidates can learn more about the program and submit their application using the link below:

Apply here

For more OFY Ambassador Programs, Click here.

The post Apply for the 2026 F4SC Ambassador Program: Become a Leader for Social Connection in Your Community appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 for AgriFood and Climate Innovation
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 is a global competition designed to identify and support startups developing innovative solutions for some of the world’s most pressing challenges in agriculture, food systems, and climate sustainability. Organized by THRIVE Agrifood and SVG Ventures, the challenge seeks entrepreneurs and startups that are building technologies capable of transforming the […]

The post Apply for the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 for AgriFood and Climate Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 is a global competition designed to identify and support startups developing innovative solutions for some of the world’s most pressing challenges in agriculture, food systems, and climate sustainability. Organized by THRIVE Agrifood and SVG Ventures, the challenge seeks entrepreneurs and startups that are building technologies capable of transforming the global agri-food ecosystem.

As climate change intensifies pressure on food systems, and as the global population continues to grow, innovation is becoming essential to ensure food security, sustainable farming practices, and resilient supply chains. Through the Global Impact Challenge, THRIVE connects startups with investors, corporate partners, and industry leaders who can help scale solutions that deliver measurable environmental and social impact.

The competition forms part of the THRIVE Global Impact Initiative, a long-term effort focused on accelerating collaboration and innovation across the agriculture and food sector. The initiative brings together startups, investors, producers, and policymakers to support technologies that improve sustainability and resilience in global food systems.

Who Should Apply

The THRIVE Challenge is open to early-stage startups and entrepreneurs from around the world who are developing solutions that address critical challenges in agriculture and food systems.

Applicants typically include startups working on innovations in areas such as:

  • Regenerative agriculture and soil health
  • Artificial intelligence and automation in agriculture
  • Water and waste management solutions
  • Clean energy and climate technologies
  • Animal health and nutrition
  • Post-harvest technologies and food innovation

The challenge welcomes companies at the seed to Series A stage, meaning applicants should have a prototype, early product, or initial market traction with the potential to scale globally.

Global participation is encouraged, and startups from any country can apply as long as their innovation aligns with the program’s focus on sustainable agriculture and climate-positive solutions.

What Selected Startups Receive

Participating in the Challenge provides startups with a powerful platform to gain visibility, mentorship, and access to investors.

Selected startups may benefit from opportunities such as:

  • Pitching their solutions to industry leaders and investors
  • Exposure to corporate partners and potential customers
  • Mentorship and coaching to strengthen their pitch and strategy
  • Participation in THRIVE’s global agri-food innovation network

Finalists present their innovations during the THRIVE Global Impact Summit in Silicon Valley, where leaders from across the global agrifood ecosystem gather to discuss trends, investment opportunities, and technological breakthroughs shaping the future of food systems.

In previous editions of the challenge, winning companies have also received investment opportunities and industry recognition that can accelerate growth and global adoption of their technologies.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Global food systems face unprecedented pressure from climate change, resource scarcity, and rising demand for sustainable production. Addressing these challenges requires bold ideas, technological innovation, and collaboration across sectors.

The Challenge helps bridge the gap between innovators and the global ecosystem of investors, corporations, and policymakers working to transform agriculture. By highlighting scalable solutions—from regenerative farming technologies to climate-smart food production—the challenge supports startups that can deliver meaningful environmental and economic impact.

For entrepreneurs, the program is more than a competition. It offers access to a global platform that can help accelerate commercialization, strengthen partnerships, and bring innovative ideas to the market faster.

Application Process and Timeline

Startups interested in participating must submit an online application outlining their innovation, impact potential, and scalability.

After applications close, selected companies may be invited to participate in Virtual Pitch Day, where they present their solutions to industry experts and investors. Finalists then advance to compete in the live Global Impact Challenge during the Global Impact Summit.

Entrepreneurs interested in applying should carefully review the eligibility criteria and prepare clear information about their technology, market potential, and measurable impact.

Applications close on 15 May 2026.

To learn more about the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge and start your application, visit the official program page.

Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website for the latest global programs and initiatives.

The post Apply for the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 for AgriFood and Climate Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Economic Empowerment Advisor Volunteer Opportunity in Uganda (Fully Funded International Service)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and empowering communities, the Economic Empowerment Advisor opportunity in Uganda offers a transformative international service experience. This program allows volunteers to support youth and community groups in developing sustainable income-generating activities while gaining valuable professional and cultural experience. Volunteers work directly with local communities to introduce microfinance […]

The post Economic Empowerment Advisor Volunteer Opportunity in Uganda (Fully Funded International Service) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and empowering communities, the Economic Empowerment Advisor opportunity in Uganda offers a transformative international service experience. This program allows volunteers to support youth and community groups in developing sustainable income-generating activities while gaining valuable professional and cultural experience.

Volunteers work directly with local communities to introduce microfinance initiatives, support small enterprises, and deliver financial literacy training that helps individuals build stable livelihoods.

Application Deadline: April 15, 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

Uganda has one of the youngest populations in the world, with more than 78% of citizens under the age of 35. Despite efforts to expand youth-focused opportunities, unemployment remains high and many young people struggle to find stable sources of income.

As an Economic Empowerment Advisor, volunteers help address this challenge by supporting entrepreneurship initiatives, strengthening community savings programs, and helping individuals develop practical job and business skills.

By working directly with community groups, volunteers contribute to sustainable economic growth while gaining meaningful international development experience.

Key Benefits of the Program

Participants receive extensive support throughout their service, including:

  • Comprehensive language, technical, and intercultural training

  • Hands-on international work experience

  • Travel to and from Uganda

  • Housing and a monthly living allowance

  • Full medical and dental coverage during service

  • A monthly accrued service award totaling $10,800 before taxes

  • Career advantages for federal hiring opportunities

  • Access to graduate school benefits and education programs

This experience not only benefits local communities but also strengthens volunteers’ professional profiles and global competencies.

What You Will Do as an Economic Empowerment Advisor

Volunteers collaborate with community members to create practical economic opportunities and strengthen financial knowledge.

Key activities include:

Supporting Community Entrepreneurship

You will help community groups identify sustainable income-generating activities such as:

  • Beekeeping

  • Soap making

  • Jam and juice production

  • Small agricultural enterprises

Delivering Financial Literacy Training

Volunteers plan and facilitate workshops on:

  • Budgeting and savings

  • Marketing strategies

  • Business planning

  • Responsible credit management

Strengthening Savings and Credit Systems

You will support groups in building savings programs and accessing low-interest microcredit opportunities.

Promoting Sustainable Agriculture

Volunteers may also support environmental and productivity initiatives such as:

  • Irrigation techniques

  • Water harvesting systems

  • Tree planting programs

Additional Community Projects

Depending on local priorities, volunteers may contribute to projects related to:

  • Nutrition education

  • Malaria prevention

  • Youth life-skills training

Training and Preparation

Before beginning fieldwork, volunteers participate in 10 weeks of intensive training that covers:

  • Microcredit mobilization

  • Community analysis

  • Project design and implementation

  • Local languages and cross-cultural communication

During this period, participants may learn local languages such as Luganda, Ateso, Lango, or Lusoga.

After training, volunteers move to a rural or peri-urban community where they collaborate directly with local residents to launch economic initiatives.

Living Conditions in Uganda

Volunteers live in rural or peri-urban communities, including villages, towns, school campuses, and clinic compounds.

Living conditions are designed to encourage cultural immersion and meaningful community integration. Volunteers gain a deep understanding of Ugandan culture while working alongside community members.

Eligibility Requirements

Competitive applicants should meet at least one of the following:

  • A Bachelor of Arts or Bachelor of Science in business or a related discipline, or

  • Five years of professional experience in business management

There are no pre-existing language requirements for this position.

Preferred qualifications include:

  • Experience in entrepreneurship, budgeting, marketing, or financial literacy

  • Basic computer skills

  • Experience training or facilitating workshops for small groups

Program Timeline

  • Application Deadline: April 15, 2026

  • Applicants Notified By: June 1, 2026

  • Departure Date: November 5, 2026

  • Training: 3 months

  • Service Duration: 2 years

Couples Participation

Couples may apply to serve together if both partners qualify for positions in different sectors. Each partner must submit a separate application.

Couples typically live together during training but may occasionally be separated for field-based assignments.

Application Process

The application process typically takes about nine months from submission to departure. During this period, candidates complete interviews, medical clearance, and legal checks before final placement.

APPLY HERE. 

Applicants interested in global development, entrepreneurship, and community empowerment are strongly encouraged to apply.

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Economic Empowerment Advisor Volunteer Opportunity in Uganda (Fully Funded International Service) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Catalyst Award 2026 – Support for Women Environmental Leaders of Color
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you a mid-career woman of color working to protect the environment, advance environmental justice, or build community resilience? The Catalyst Award is a prestigious initiative from Rachel’s Network that recognizes and supports women environmental leaders of color who are shaping a healthier, safer, and more just world. Each year, the Catalyst Award celebrates women […]

The post Apply for the Catalyst Award 2026 – Support for Women Environmental Leaders of Color appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a mid-career woman of color working to protect the environment, advance environmental justice, or build community resilience? The Catalyst Award is a prestigious initiative from Rachel’s Network that recognizes and supports women environmental leaders of color who are shaping a healthier, safer, and more just world.

Each year, the Catalyst Award celebrates women environmental champions by providing financial grants, wraparound leadership support, and public recognition to nominees whose work demonstrates tangible impact in their communities, fields, and broader movements.

About the Catalyst Award

Established by Rachel’s Network — a nonprofit community dedicated to empowering women leaders at the intersection of environmental advocacy, philanthropy, and leadership — the Catalyst Award addresses the historic underrepresentation and under-funding of women of color in environmental leadership roles.

The award is designed to celebrate women who are building a healthier planet through courageous leadership, strategic action, and community transformation.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Environmental leadership is essential to addressing climate change, biodiversity loss, pollution, and environmental injustice. Yet historically, women — especially women of color — have faced systemic barriers to funding, recognition, and access to networks that can accelerate their impact.

Data shows that women and girls of color receive a disproportionately small share of foundation funding, despite being among the most active and effective environmental advocates worldwide. The Catalyst Award directly addresses this gap by centering mid-career women leaders of color and providing them with the resources, recognition, and leadership support they deserve.

By elevating women environmental leaders, the award not only advances individual careers but also strengthens broader movements for environmental justice, community resilience, and equitable climate action. Recipients have worked on issues ranging from sustainable agriculture and water justice to climate advocacy, conservation, and food systems reform — illustrating the diversity and interconnected nature of environmental leadership.

This award highlights the value of representation and investment in women of color who bring critical perspectives, innovative solutions, and deep community connections to environmental work.

Eligibility and Who Should Apply

The Catalyst Award is for women of color — including individuals who identify as non-binary, gender expansive, or otherwise — who are at the midpoint of their careers with significant environmental leadership experience and potential for further impact.

While women from diverse professional backgrounds are encouraged to apply, the following types of leaders are representative of past nominees and awardees:

  • Organizers defending communities and land rights
  • Educators nurturing young advocates
  • Farmers advancing food justice and sustainable agriculture
  • Journalists and documentarians amplifying environmental narratives
  • Faith leaders elevating moral imperatives for environmental action
  • Scientists and researchers advancing planetary understanding
  • Entrepreneurs driving equitable social and economic change

Applicants do not need to be nominated to apply; nomination is optional but may help signal support. Once nominations are submitted, nominees will be contacted to complete the full application.

The program is open to women environmental leaders of color who are U.S. residents — including those in American Indian, Alaska Native areas, and U.S. territories — regardless of citizenship status.

Award Benefits and Support

Each year, the Catalyst Award provides recipients with:

  • A personal grant to support leadership development and personal goals
  • An organizational grant (if applicable) to support the nominee’s affiliated nonprofit or initiative
  • Wraparound leadership services including mentoring, networking, and cohort engagement
  • Public recognition that elevates the awardees’ visibility and influence within environmental and advocacy communities

The Catalyst Award provides significant financial support and recognition to help women leaders scale their impact and strengthen their movements.

Winners receive personal grants of $15,000 and organizational grants of $10,000 (if applicable), along with wraparound leadership services and public recognition.

Awardees are encouraged to engage in cohort programming such as webinars, retreats, and community events, and often participate as mentors or selection committee members in subsequent cycles.

Application Timeline & How to Apply

Deadline for submissions: 31 March 2026. Learn more and apply on the official website.

To be ready, prospective applicants should prepare a strong narrative that articulates their leadership journey, environmental impact, and vision for future change. Nominations are optional but can serve as a supportive endorsement.

Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website.

The post Apply for the Catalyst Award 2026 – Support for Women Environmental Leaders of Color appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 12 Positions Open with Green Climate Fund – Apply Today!
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you looking for a career where your work contributes directly to tackling climate change? The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is currently inviting professionals from around the world to apply for multiple open positions across its departments, offering the opportunity to work at the forefront of global climate finance and sustainable development. The Green Climate […]

The post 12 Positions Open with Green Climate Fund – Apply Today! appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you looking for a career where your work contributes directly to tackling climate change? The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is currently inviting professionals from around the world to apply for multiple open positions across its departments, offering the opportunity to work at the forefront of global climate finance and sustainable development.

The Green Climate Fund plays a central role in supporting developing countries as they work to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and build climate-resilient societies. Through its global funding mechanisms and partnerships, the organization supports projects that address climate challenges while promoting sustainable economic growth.

For professionals interested in climate policy, finance, environmental sustainability, communications, governance, or international development, these openings offer a chance to contribute to one of the most significant climate institutions in the world.

About the Green Climate Fund

The Green Climate Fund was established under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) as a global funding mechanism to help developing countries respond to climate change.

Its mission is to support both climate mitigation—reducing greenhouse gas emissions—and climate adaptation, which helps communities and ecosystems adapt to changing climate conditions.

Through partnerships with governments, international organizations, development banks, and civil society groups, the Green Climate Fund finances projects that promote renewable energy, climate-resilient infrastructure, sustainable agriculture, and environmental protection.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is one of the most pressing challenges facing the world today. Addressing it requires coordinated action across governments, financial institutions, and international organizations.

The Green Climate Fund plays a critical role in mobilizing billions of dollars in climate finance to support countries that are most vulnerable to climate impacts. By funding projects that strengthen resilience and reduce emissions, the organization helps accelerate the global transition toward sustainable development.

Working with the Green Climate Fund allows professionals to contribute directly to initiatives that support renewable energy, climate-smart agriculture, sustainable cities, and ecosystem protection.

For individuals seeking careers that combine professional growth with global impact, these positions offer a meaningful opportunity to participate in shaping the future of climate action.

Available Positions

The current recruitment call includes multiple job opportunities across different professional areas, offering roles suited to professionals with diverse skills and experience.

Examples of potential areas include:

  • Climate finance and investment
  • Environmental policy and program management
  • Risk management and compliance
  • Communications and stakeholder engagement
  • Data analysis and research
  • Information technology and digital systems
  • Legal and governance
  • Administrative and operational support

These positions provide the opportunity to work with a global team of experts and contribute to projects that support climate solutions around the world.

Because the Green Climate Fund operates at an international level, many roles require strong collaboration skills and the ability to work in multicultural environments.

Who Should Apply

The Green Climate Fund positions are open to qualified professionals from different backgrounds who are passionate about climate action and sustainable development.

Applicants typically come from fields such as:

  • Environmental science
  • Climate policy
  • Economics and finance
  • International development
  • Public administration
  • Communications
  • Law and governance

Candidates should demonstrate a strong commitment to addressing climate challenges and contributing to global development efforts.

Depending on the specific role, applicants may also need relevant professional experience, technical expertise, or academic qualifications in their field.

Application Timeline

Application deadlines vary depending on the position, but all of the applications will close by the 25 of March 2026.

Interested applicants should review the available roles and submit their applications through the Green Climate Fund careers page.

You can explore the available positions and apply through the official listing.

Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website and find more opportunities worldwide!

The post 12 Positions Open with Green Climate Fund – Apply Today! appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) Doctoral Focal Award offers a fully funded PhD opportunity for students interested in exploring the intersection between mental health, sustainable living, and climate-related social challenges. This prestigious doctoral program is supported by the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and brings together several leading UK universities […]

The post Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) Doctoral Focal Award offers a fully funded PhD opportunity for students interested in exploring the intersection between mental health, sustainable living, and climate-related social challenges. This prestigious doctoral program is supported by the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and brings together several leading UK universities and regional partners to train the next generation of researchers, policymakers, and creative practitioners.

The program will begin in 2026 and is open to both UK and international applicants. It provides four years of financial support, advanced training, and opportunities to collaborate with organizations across the health, sustainability, arts, and community sectors.

Applicants must submit their applications before March 31, 2026.

About the EM-SLAM Programme

The East Midlands Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing (EM-SLAM) initiative is a collaborative research program designed to address the growing psychological and social challenges associated with climate change and ecological uncertainty.

The program is jointly delivered by a partnership of universities across the East Midlands region of the United Kingdom, including:

  • Loughborough University

  • De Montfort University

  • University of Leicester

  • Nottingham Trent University

These institutions work together with a range of non-academic partners, including organizations involved in public health, environmental sustainability, community development, and the creative arts.

The primary aim of EM-SLAM is to explore how societies can support individuals and communities in living sustainably while maintaining mental wellbeing and social resilience in the face of environmental crises.

Through interdisciplinary research and storytelling approaches, the program seeks to generate knowledge that can inform policy, inspire communities, and support healthier relationships between people and the environment.

Research Focus: Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing

One of the defining characteristics of the EM-SLAM doctoral program is its focus on the intersection between mental health and environmental sustainability.

Climate change and ecological disruption are increasingly affecting the psychological wellbeing of individuals and communities. Concepts such as climate anxiety, ecological grief, and environmental distress are becoming more prominent in academic research and public discourse.

The EM-SLAM programme aims to investigate these issues through innovative research that highlights human experiences, narratives, and community perspectives.

Doctoral researchers will explore how people can:

  • Adapt to ecological uncertainty while maintaining mental resilience

  • Build sustainable lifestyles that support both environmental health and human wellbeing

  • Develop meaningful connections with nature and community

  • Address climate anxiety and ecological grief through storytelling and social engagement

Researchers are encouraged to develop projects that incorporate qualitative and interpretative research methods, with storytelling serving as a central framework for understanding lived experiences.

Interdisciplinary Research Environment

The EM-SLAM programme provides a highly interdisciplinary research environment that bridges multiple academic fields.

Students will receive supervision and mentorship from experts in areas such as:

  • Social sciences

  • Arts and humanities

  • Environmental studies

  • Public health

  • Cultural and creative industries

This interdisciplinary approach ensures that doctoral researchers develop a broad perspective on sustainability and mental health challenges while learning how to collaborate across different academic and professional disciplines.

Students will also work closely with external partners, allowing them to connect their research to real-world policy and community initiatives.

Why Apply for the EM-SLAM Doctoral Studentship

The EM-SLAM PhD studentship offers a wide range of academic and professional benefits designed to support early-career researchers.

Advanced Research Training

Students will gain advanced research skills through a comprehensive training programme that includes methodological workshops, interdisciplinary seminars, and professional development activities.

This training is designed to prepare participants for careers in academia, policymaking, creative industries, and public sector organizations.

Collaborative Research Opportunities

Participants will work with researchers and professionals from different sectors to co-design projects that address real-world social and environmental challenges.

This collaborative approach allows doctoral researchers to develop research that is both academically rigorous and socially impactful.

Professional Placements and Partnerships

A key component of the EM-SLAM program is the opportunity to undertake short placements with partner organizations.

These placements may involve collaboration with:

  • Public health organizations

  • Environmental sustainability initiatives

  • Cultural and creative institutions

  • Community development programs

Such experiences allow researchers to apply their academic work in practical settings and gain valuable professional experience.

Inclusive Research Community

Students will join a vibrant and supportive cohort of researchers from diverse backgrounds.

The programme prioritizes equity, diversity, and inclusion, actively encouraging applications from individuals who may be underrepresented in academia.

Funding and Financial Support

The EM-SLAM Doctoral Focal Award provides four years of fully funded doctoral support.

The funding package includes:

  • Full coverage of UK tuition fees

  • A tax-free stipend to support living expenses

  • Funding for training and professional development

  • Financial support for conference participation

  • Opportunities for research collaboration and placements

The programme is funded through the Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC), one of the UK’s most prestigious research funding bodies.

While the studentship primarily covers UK fees, international students are also eligible, although only a limited number of awards are available for applicants from outside the UK.

According to UK Research and Innovation guidelines, up to 30 percent of the studentships may be awarded to international candidates.

Entry Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate strong academic preparation and a clear research interest in the themes of sustainable living and mental wellbeing.

Typical requirements include:

  • A background in arts and humanities or social sciences

  • A Master’s degree in a relevant discipline

  • A first or upper second class honours undergraduate degree from a UK university or equivalent qualification

Applicants with a lower second class honours degree may still be considered if they hold a Master’s degree with Merit level or equivalent.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose first language is not English must demonstrate proficiency through an approved English language test.

The required score is:

  • IELTS overall score of 6.5

  • Minimum 6.0 in each component (reading, writing, listening, and speaking)

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here

The post Fully Funded EM-SLAM PhD Studentship 2026 in the UK: Research Sustainable Living, Climate Anxiety, and Mental Wellbeing appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Scholarships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity for young leaders around the world to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, one of the most influential global youth forums dedicated to sustainable development and international cooperation. The event will take place August 12–14, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, bringing together emerging leaders, […]

The post AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Scholarships 2026 offer an exceptional opportunity for young leaders around the world to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, one of the most influential global youth forums dedicated to sustainable development and international cooperation.

The event will take place August 12–14, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, bringing together emerging leaders, policymakers, researchers, and youth advocates to collaborate on solutions to some of the most pressing global challenges.

This initiative is organized by AFS Intercultural Programs in partnership with AstraZeneca, through its Young Health Programme, which focuses on empowering youth to create healthier, more sustainable communities worldwide.

The scholarship aims to support young people who are already working to advance health equity, climate and nature action, and resilient health systems.

The application deadline has not been officially specified, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible once the application portal opens.

About the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP)

The Young Health Programme (YHP) is AstraZeneca’s global community investment initiative focused on preventing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and promoting health equity, particularly among young people.

Through partnerships with civil society organizations, youth leaders, and international institutions, the program aims to empower the next generation to become advocates for better health systems and sustainable development.

By supporting youth initiatives, the program addresses the growing global health challenges associated with chronic diseases, environmental risks, and healthcare inequality.

The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships are part of this broader mission to equip youth with the knowledge, networks, and leadership tools necessary to drive positive change.

About the AFS Youth Assembly

The AFS Youth Assembly is a global forum that convenes young leaders from around the world to discuss innovative solutions to international challenges.

Hosted in Geneva, Switzerland, a city known for its strong diplomatic presence and international institutions, the assembly provides a unique environment for dialogue, collaboration, and knowledge exchange.

The 2026 edition will gather youth advocates working across sectors including health, climate, sustainable development, education, and social justice.

Participants will have the opportunity to:

  • Engage in high-level discussions with global experts and policymakers

  • Participate in workshops and collaborative sessions focused on leadership and innovation

  • Share their experiences and projects with an international audience

  • Build a global network of like-minded changemakers

The Youth Assembly is designed to amplify youth voices in international decision-making processes and encourage collaborative solutions to global challenges.

Focus Areas of the AstraZeneca Scholarship

Applicants for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship are expected to demonstrate commitment and leadership in at least one of the following key areas:

Advancing Health Equity

One of the core priorities of the program is addressing inequalities in healthcare access and outcomes.

Scholars working in this area focus on preventing non-communicable diseases (NCDs) among young people. These diseases include:

  • Lung diseases

  • Cardiovascular diseases

  • Cancer

  • Type 2 diabetes

  • Chronic kidney disease

Young advocates supporting individuals living with rare diseases are also encouraged to apply.

The goal is to promote early prevention strategies and ensure equitable access to healthcare services across communities.

Climate and Nature Action

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major threat to global health.

Young leaders working in climate and environmental advocacy can apply if their work focuses on reducing the harmful effects of climate change on public health and well-being.

Applicants may be involved in initiatives such as:

  • Climate awareness campaigns

  • Environmental health advocacy

  • Sustainable community programs

  • Climate policy engagement

These efforts are essential to protecting vulnerable populations and ensuring long-term health outcomes.

Building Resilient Health Systems

Another critical priority is strengthening health systems to respond to both current and future health challenges.

Applicants working in this field may focus on:

  • Strengthening healthcare infrastructure

  • Promoting early disease detection and prevention

  • Improving community-based healthcare programs

  • Advocating for policy reforms that enhance healthcare accessibility

Resilient health systems are essential to ensuring societies can respond effectively to both emerging diseases and ongoing health crises.

What the Scholarship Includes

Recipients of the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship will receive support to attend the AFS Youth Assembly 2026 in Geneva.

The scholarship is designed to ensure that young leaders from diverse backgrounds can participate in the event regardless of financial barriers.

Benefits typically include:

  • Access to the AFS Youth Assembly global conference

  • Participation in leadership workshops and panel discussions

  • Networking opportunities with international experts and policymakers

  • Collaboration with youth leaders from around the world

Scholars also gain international exposure and recognition for their work in health, climate, and sustainability.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Today’s world faces a complex intersection of challenges including rising chronic disease, climate change, and unequal healthcare access.

These issues disproportionately affect young people and marginalized communities.

By supporting youth leaders who are actively addressing these problems, the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme seeks to build a generation capable of driving systemic change.

Participation in the Youth Assembly enables young leaders to:

  • Share innovative ideas and projects

  • Influence global discussions on health and sustainability

  • Develop leadership and advocacy skills

  • Build long-term collaborations with global changemakers

For many participants, the Youth Assembly becomes a launching platform for international careers in policy, research, public health, and global development.

How to Apply

Applicants interested in the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarship should prepare a strong application highlighting their work, leadership experience, and commitment to improving global health and sustainability.

Candidates should clearly demonstrate:

  • Their involvement in projects related to health equity, climate action, or resilient health systems

  • Their vision for creating positive change in their communities

  • Their motivation to participate in the Youth Assembly

Applications must be submitted through the official AFS Youth Assembly scholarship portal.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to regularly check the official website for application updates and submission guidelines.

For more information click here 

Apply here

Discover more opportunities here

The post AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Attend the AFS Youth Assembly in Geneva appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
